rulings.Case Title: LINDA CATRON VS. COUNTRYWIDE BANK, FSB ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Notice And Motion To Reconsider Order 3-27-24 Of Plaintiff'S Notice And Motion To Reconsider Defendant'S Bank Of America Na Motion To Dismiss 11/15/2023 And Court Error Defendant Not Party To Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Motion calendar for August 8, 2025, line 1. Plaintiff's Motion to Reconsider is OFF CALENDAR. Notice of Stay of Proceedings on file. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tent ative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: AIRA DE VERA ET AL VS. BENJAMIN SHAWN BERRY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enter Judgment Pursuant To Settlement Agreement
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Motion calendar for August 8, 2025, line 2. Plaintiff's Motion to Enter Judgment Pursuant to Settlement Agreement is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. There are several issues with the moving papers. The Proof of Service is dated June 12, 2024, attesting to service on June 12, 2025. Plaintiff does not have competent evidence of a breach because he does not explain how he has personal knowledge that Defendant "did not pay" plaintiffs. Counsel provides no dates or exhibits of his attempts to contact or locate opposing counsel, no explanation for why Plaintiff waited 9 months after the purported breach to bring this motion, and also appears to have signed this agreement approving it as to form 10 days after the first payment was due. Finally, the settlement agreement seems to contradict itself because it requires an initial payment of $7,500 "to each Plaintiff," which would satisfy the entire settlement amount, followed by additional monthly payments. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tent ative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: PETER WILMS VS. JACK RHIEL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Motion calendar for August 8, 2025, line 3. Plaintiff's Motion to be Relieved as Counsel is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. Counsel did not attach a declaration in support of the motion. "The motion to be relieved as counsel must be accompanied by a declaration on the Declaration in Support of Attorney's Motion to Be Relieved as Counsel-Civil (form MC-052). The declaration must state in general terms and without compromising the confidentiality of the attorney-client relationship why a motion under Code of Civil Procedure section 284(2) is brought instead of filing a consent under Code of Civil Procedure section 284(1)." (CRC 3.1362(c)). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tent ative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JULIE SWAN ET AL VS. ABACUS PROPERTY MANAGEMENT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Motion calendar for August 8, 2025, line 6. Defendant's Motion to Quash Service of Summons or Stay or Dismiss is OFF CALENDAR. The motion to quash is untimely. A motion to quash must be made "on or before the last day of his or her time to plead." (C.C.P. Section 418.10(a)). A Defendant's last day to plead is 30 days after service is deemed complete, and Defendant Abacus was served with the Summons and FAC on February 10, 2025. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tent ative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
